hero.title: How to Segment an Image Online: Use Cases & Step-by-Step Guide
hero.subtitle: Follow this tutorial to segment a photo online, lock Default / 4:5 / 3:4 / 1:1 ratios, drag the crop box, and export 3, 6, 9+ slices without installing software.
hero.coverAlt: Tutorial hero showing image slices and ratio presets

intro.title: Why learn how to segment an image online?
paragraphs: Segmenting an image online helps bloggers, educators, and designers control load speed, sync storytelling, and prepare print-ready files without opening heavy desktop apps. Lock a ratio, drag a crop box, and the tool generates equal slices automatically.

Below you'll find the most common reasons to segment an image online plus four practical ratio workflows inside AI Image Splitter. Everything runs locally in your browser. When you're ready, open AI Image Splitter and start segmenting an image online in minutes.

cta.label: Open AI Image Splitter and start segmenting images online

scenarios.title: Typical scenarios for segmenting an image online

items.title: Web & blog galleries
items.description: Segment a large hero into smaller slices so each tile loads progressively on long-form articles.

items.title: Product walkthroughs
items.description: Segment posters or diagrams into multiple slides for PDF handouts, PPT decks, or multi-screen demos.

items.title: A4 printing & collage
items.description: Segment a single poster into A4 sheets that can be printed, trimmed, and assembled offline.

items.title: Long story or scroll experience
items.description: Segment tall artwork into equal sections for blogs, comics, or scroll-style stories.

cases.title: Tutorial: how to segment an image online with four ratios

items.label: Default ratio
items.heading: Segment images online in the Default ratio for blog galleries
items.description: Keep the original aspect ratio when you simply need lighter slices for blogs or documentation.
steps: Upload your image and keep the ratio on Default.
Choose the grid (for example 2x3) and drag the crop box until the framing feels right.
Click Split Image to segment the file online and download each slice (single files or a ZIP).

items.label: 4:5 ratio
items.heading: Segment vertical stories online with the 4:5 ratio
items.description: Lock the canvas to 4:5 for scroll experiences, tall posters, or any vertical storyboard.
steps: Upload the artwork and choose the 4:5 ratio.
Set a grid like 1x3 or 1x4 depending on how many stacked slices you need.
Preview and export. If you require Instagram feed-specific layouts, visit igridmaker.com.

items.label: 3:4 ratio
items.heading: Segment balanced walkthroughs with the 3:4 ratio
items.description: 3:4 works well for PDF or PPT slides where you need evenly balanced frames.
steps: Upload the poster and select the 3:4 ratio.
Pick a grid (such as 2x2) that matches the number of slides you want to showcase.
Split the image online and drop each slice into your deck or document.

items.label: 1:1 ratio
items.heading: Segment square slices online for collages and Bento layouts
items.description: Use 1:1 when you want perfectly even squares for collages, Bento grids, or printable tiles.
steps: Upload the original image and pick the 1:1 ratio.
Set a grid such as 3x3 or 4x4, drag the crop box, and preview.
Download the squares for collages, Bento layouts, or A4 tile printing. For Instagram-specific puzzles, head to igridmaker.com.

faq.title: FAQ about segmenting an image online

items.question: Do I need to install software to segment an image online?
items.answer: No. Upload in the browser, pick a ratio and grid, click Split Image, and download the slices. It's 100% online and free.

items.question: Is there a limit to how many slices I can segment online?
items.answer: You can create as many slices as your image resolution allows. Adjust the row/column combination to match your use case.

items.question: Will online segmentation reduce image quality?
items.answer: No. The locked crop box only controls the framing, so exported slices keep the original resolution and stay print-ready.

items.question: Which formats can I export after segmenting images online?
items.answer: JPG, PNG, WebP, and BMP are supported. Download individual files or a ZIP bundle when you finish segmenting.

items.question: Where can I build Instagram feed-specific grids?
items.answer: Use this tutorial for general segmentation. For Instagram feed puzzles or grid layouts, visit our sister site igridmaker.com.
